title: ranunculus lyallii var. traversii
intro: Ranunculus lyallii var. traversii, also known as the Travers' buttercup, is a rare and unique plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pale yellow flowers and slender foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Ranunculus lyallii var. traversii are said to include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as wound healing and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the roots or leaves in hot water, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
